comment_text.sn3.25:2.5: This extraordinary threat looms also in an Upaniṣadic passage: “Even if both the mountain ranges, the southern and the northern, were to rush at him determined to level him, they would not succeed in leveling him” (Kauṣītaki Upaniṣad 2.13, translation Olivelle).
comment_text.sn3.25:4.13: Bhūmigata is explained as “underground” at Bu Pj 2:4.2.1 (there bhūmaṭṭha). | Vehāsaṭṭha is explained as “above ground” at Bu Pj 2:4.5.1. | Upalāpetuṁ (“bribe”) is a means by which Vassakāra suggests the Vajjis can be overcome (DN 16:1.5.8, AN 7.22:12.3).

root_text.sn3.25:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.25:1.3: “handa kuto nu tvaṁ, mahārāja, āgacchasi divā divassā”ti?
root_text.sn3.25:1.4: “Yāni tāni, bhante, raññaṁ khattiyānaṁ muddhāvasittānaṁ issariyamadamattānaṁ kāmagedhapariyuṭṭhitānaṁ janapadatthāvariyappattānaṁ mahantaṁ pathavimaṇḍalaṁ abhivijiya ajjhāvasantānaṁ rājakaraṇīyāni bhavanti, tesu khvāhaṁ, etarahi ussukkamāpanno”ti.
root_text.sn3.25:2.2: idha te puriso āgaccheyya puratthimāya disāya saddhāyiko paccayiko.
root_text.sn3.25:2.4: ‘yagghe, mahārāja, jāneyyāsi, ahaṁ āgacchāmi puratthimāya disāya.
root_text.sn3.25:2.5: Tatthaddasaṁ mahantaṁ pabbataṁ abbhasamaṁ, sabbe pāṇe nippothento āgacchati.
root_text.sn3.25:2.6: Yaṁ te, mahārāja, karaṇīyaṁ, taṁ karohī’ti.
root_text.sn3.25:2.7: Atha dutiyo puriso āgaccheyya pacchimāya disāya …pe…
root_text.sn3.25:2.8: atha tatiyo puriso āgaccheyya uttarāya disāya …
root_text.sn3.25:2.9: atha catuttho puriso āgaccheyya dakkhiṇāya disāya saddhāyiko paccayiko.
root_text.sn3.25:2.11: ‘yagghe, mahārāja, jāneyyāsi, ahaṁ āgacchāmi dakkhiṇāya disāya.
root_text.sn3.25:2.12: Tatthaddasaṁ mahantaṁ pabbataṁ abbhasamaṁ sabbe pāṇe nippothento āgacchati.
root_text.sn3.25:2.13: Yaṁ te, mahārāja, karaṇīyaṁ taṁ karohī’ti.
root_text.sn3.25:2.14: Evarūpe te, mahārāja, mahati mahabbhaye samuppanne dāruṇe manussakkhaye dullabhe manussatte kimassa karaṇīyan”ti?
root_text.sn3.25:3.1: “Evarūpe me, bhante, mahati mahabbhaye samuppanne dāruṇe manussakkhaye dullabhe manussatte kimassa karaṇīyaṁ aññatra dhammacariyāya aññatra samacariyāya aññatra kusalakiriyāya aññatra puññakiriyāyā”ti?
root_text.sn3.25:4.1: “Ārocemi kho te, mahārāja, paṭivedemi kho te, mahārāja, adhivattati kho taṁ, mahārāja, jarāmaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn3.25:4.2: Adhivattamāne ce te, mahārāja, jarāmaraṇe kimassa karaṇīyan”ti?
root_text.sn3.25:4.3: “Adhivattamāne ca me, bhante, jarāmaraṇe kimassa karaṇīyaṁ aññatra dhammacariyāya samacariyāya kusalakiriyāya puññakiriyāya?
root_text.sn3.25:4.4: Yāni tāni, bhante, raññaṁ khattiyānaṁ muddhāvasittānaṁ issariyamadamattānaṁ kāmagedhapariyuṭṭhitānaṁ janapadatthāvariyappattānaṁ mahantaṁ pathavimaṇḍalaṁ abhivijiya ajjhāvasantānaṁ hatthiyuddhāni bhavanti;
root_text.sn3.25:4.5: tesampi, bhante, hatthiyuddhānaṁ natthi gati natthi visayo adhivattamāne jarāmaraṇe.
root_text.sn3.25:4.6: Yānipi tāni, bhante, raññaṁ khattiyānaṁ muddhāvasittānaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn3.25:4.10: tesampi, bhante, pattiyuddhānaṁ natthi gati natthi visayo adhivattamāne jarāmaraṇe.
root_text.sn3.25:4.11: Santi kho pana, bhante, imasmiṁ rājakule mantino mahāmattā, ye pahonti āgate paccatthike mantehi bhedayituṁ.
root_text.sn3.25:4.12: Tesampi, bhante, mantayuddhānaṁ natthi gati natthi visayo adhivattamāne jarāmaraṇe.
root_text.sn3.25:4.13: Saṁvijjati kho pana, bhante, imasmiṁ rājakule pahūtaṁ hiraññasuvaṇṇaṁ bhūmigatañceva vehāsaṭṭhañca, yena mayaṁ pahoma āgate paccatthike dhanena upalāpetuṁ.
root_text.sn3.25:4.14: Tesampi, bhante, dhanayuddhānaṁ natthi gati natthi visayo adhivattamāne jarāmaraṇe.
root_text.sn3.25:4.15: Adhivattamāne ca me, bhante, jarāmaraṇe kimassa karaṇīyaṁ aññatra dhammacariyāya samacariyāya kusalakiriyāya puññakiriyāyā”ti?
root_text.sn3.25:5.2: Adhivattamāne jarāmaraṇe kimassa karaṇīyaṁ aññatra dhammacariyāya samacariyāya kusalakiriyāya puññakiriyāyā”ti?

translation_text.sn3.25:1.2: King Pasenadi of Kosala sat to one side, and the Buddha said to him,
translation_text.sn3.25:1.3: “So, great king, where are you coming from in the middle of the day?”
translation_text.sn3.25:1.4: “Sir, there are anointed aristocratic kings who are infatuated with authority, and obsessed with greed for sensual pleasures. They have attained stability in the country, occupying a vast conquered territory. Today I have been busy fulfilling the duties of such kings.”
translation_text.sn3.25:2.2: Suppose a trustworthy and dependable man were to come from the east.
translation_text.sn3.25:2.4: ‘Please great king, you should know this. I come from the east.
translation_text.sn3.25:2.5: There I saw a huge mountain that reached the clouds. And it was coming this way, crushing all creatures.
translation_text.sn3.25:2.6: So then, great king, do what you must!’
translation_text.sn3.25:2.7: Then a second trustworthy and dependable man were to come from the west …
translation_text.sn3.25:2.9: and a fourth from the south.
translation_text.sn3.25:2.11: ‘Please great king, you should know this. I come from the south.
translation_text.sn3.25:2.12: There I saw a huge mountain that reached the clouds. And it was coming this way, crushing all creatures.
translation_text.sn3.25:2.13: So then, great king, do what you must!’
translation_text.sn3.25:2.14: Should such a dire threat arise—a grim loss of human life, when human birth is so rare—what would you do?”
translation_text.sn3.25:3.1: “Sir, what could I do but practice the teachings, practice morality, doing skillful and good actions?”
translation_text.sn3.25:4.1: “I tell you, great king, I announce to you: old age and death are advancing upon you.
translation_text.sn3.25:4.2: Since old age and death are advancing upon you, what would you do?”
translation_text.sn3.25:4.3: “Sir, what can I do but practice the teachings, practice morality, doing skillful and good actions?
translation_text.sn3.25:4.4: Sir, there are anointed aristocratic kings who are infatuated with authority, and obsessed with greed for sensual pleasures. They have attained stability in the country, occupying a vast conquered territory. Such kings engage in battles of elephants,
translation_text.sn3.25:4.10: But there is no place, no domain for such battles when old age and death are advancing.
translation_text.sn3.25:4.11: In this royal court there are ministers of wise counsel who are capable of dividing an approaching enemy by wise counsel.
translation_text.sn3.25:4.12: But there is no place, no domain for such diplomatic battles when old age and death are advancing.
translation_text.sn3.25:4.13: In this royal court there is abundant gold, both coined and uncoined, stored above and below ground. Using this wealth we can bribe an approaching enemy.
translation_text.sn3.25:4.14: But there is no place, no domain for such monetary battles when old age and death are advancing.
translation_text.sn3.25:4.15: When old age and death are advancing, what can I do but practice the teachings, practice morality, doing skillful and good actions?”
translation_text.sn3.25:5.1: “That’s so true, great king! That’s so true!
translation_text.sn3.25:5.2: When old age and death are advancing, what can you do but practice the teachings, practice morality, doing skillful and good actions?”
translation_text.sn3.25:5.3: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn3.25:5.4: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn3.25:6.2: of solid rock pressing against the sky
translation_text.sn3.25:7.1: So too old age and death
translation_text.sn3.25:8.1: There’s nowhere for elephants to take a stand,
translation_text.sn3.25:8.4: by diplomatic battles or by wealth.
translation_text.sn3.25:9.4: in the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn3.25:10.4: and departs to rejoice in heaven.”
translation_text.sn3.25:11.5: The Linked Discourses with the Kosalan are completed.

variant_text.sn3.25:10.1: dhammaṁ cari → dhammacārī (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed); dhammacāri (pts2ed)

reference_text.sn3.25:1.1: cck15.138, csp1ed12.101, csp2ed12.101, ms12S1_755, msdiv136, ndp12.100, pts-vp-pli2ed1.225
reference_text.sn3.25:2.1: dr15.138, ms12S1_756, pts-vp-pli1ed1.101, pts-vp-pli2ed1.226, sya15.147, vri23.120
